items.summary: In useQueries.ts and useActor.ts, diagnose and fix the root cause of the 'Actor not available' error that surfaces on the New Client page (ClientDetailPage in create mode) when the user is authenticated via Internet Identity. The actor must be initialized and ready before any mutation (createClient) is attempted. If the actor is not yet ready, the page must show a loading state rather than an error banner. Once the actor is available, the createClient mutation must fire successfully when the Create button is pressed.

In useActor.ts, ensure the actor is created as soon as an authenticated identity is available and that React Query caches it so subsequent hook calls on the same page receive the same actor instance without re-triggering initialization. The actor query must not return undefined or null once the identity is confirmed; if initialization is in progress it must return a loading state, not an error state.

In ClientDetailPage.tsx, guard the createClient mutation call so it only fires when the actor is confirmed non-null. If the actor is not yet available when the Create button is pressed, show an inline loading indicator on the button rather than propagating an error. Wire the useCreateClient mutation from useQueries.ts so it receives the actor reference correctly and does not fall back to a no-op when the actor is undefined.
